Then read the file to see if an 'auth required pam_wheel.so use_uid' line exists (with or without leading whitespace). If not, then either uncomment existing commented line or add new line. - Read /etc/pam.d/su using read_file, search for 'pam_wheel.so use_uid' with pattern ignoring whitespace before hash. If found with '#', use sed to uncomment. If found without '#', do nothing. We'll simply add at end of file.

- Use sed -i with regex: `^\s*#?\s*auth\s+required\s+pam_wheel\.so\s+use_uid$`. But to uncomment if it is commented, we can substitution: `s/^\s*#\s*\(auth\s+required\s+pam_wheel\.so\s+use_uid\)/\1/`. But we want to consider optional whitespace before '#'. Use `s/^\s*#\s*\(auth\s+required\s+pam_wheel\.so\s+use_uid\)/\1/`. That uncomments the line. We'll use sed -i.

- Then check again for existence; if not found, append.
